channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Stop burning money in 2026: How to find and cancel your unneeded subscriptions easily
item.description: The new year often brings sticker shock. A glance at our bank statements and credit card bills shows just how much we spent during the holidays, serving as a painful reminder that with the festivities behind us, we should work on getting our expenditures under control.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: The 14 global trends that will shape the climate in 2026
item.description: The future of the energy transition is being shaped by President Donald Trump’s climate policy rollbacks that favor fossil fuels — but also the life support for renewables and batteries paradoxically coming from data center power demand.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How AI transforms agriculture in the rural world
item.description: It all started with a sewing machine. Ramón Álvarez de Arriba was one of the many Asturian immigrants who sought their fortunes in America in the 19th century.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Back to the future: The most popular JavaScript stories and themes of 2025
item.description: Artificial intelligence and its promise to revolutionize programming—and possibly overthrow human sovereignty—is a central story of the post-Covid world. But for JavaScript developers, it is only one of the forces contending for center stage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 47 awesome Android app discoveries from 2025
item.description: It’s the calm before the storm. Today, on New Year’s Day, we have a brief moment to pause and prepare — and set ourselves up for success. From a tech perspective, that means taking the time to clean up and optimize your smartphone setup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 11 in-demand cloud roles companies are hiring for
item.description: Organizations continue to invest heavily in cloud solutions heading into 2026, with the goal of improving employee productivity (31%), accelerating adoption of AI and ML (13%), and improving security and governance in the organization (30%), according to the 2025 CIO Cloud Computing Study from Found
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is cloud computing? From infrastructure to autonomous, agentic-driven ecosystems
item.description: Cloud computing continues to be the platform of choice for large applications and a driver of innovation in enterprise technology. Gartner forecasts public cloud spending alone to the public cloud services market alone will reach $1.42 trillion in current U.S.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI tightens AI rules for teens but concerns remain
item.description: OpenAI announces new teen safety rules for ChatGPT users under 18, blocking romantic roleplay and requiring extra caution on body image topics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2026: The year we stop trusting any single cloud
item.description: For more than a decade, many considered cloud outages a theoretical risk, something to address on a whiteboard and then quietly deprioritize during cost cuts. In 2025, this risk became real.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SoftBank to acquire DigitalBridge for $4 billion
item.description: SoftBank Group will acquire digital infrastructure investor DigitalBridge Group in a deal valued at $4 billion, the companies said on Monday, as the Japanese investment firm looks to deepen its AI-related portfolio.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The 27 best new apps of 2025
item.description: As always, many of this year’s best apps are ones you’ve probably never heard of. Sure, there are some big names on this list, particularly in the buzzy field of artificial intelligence, but the real standouts of 2025 innovated on a smaller scale.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: More than $32 billion in U.S. clean energy projects were canceled in 2025
item.description: Two years ago, a $575 million battery factory planned in St. Louis, Missouri, was set to be the first large-scale lithium iron phosphate (LFP) facility in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: 700Credit data breach exposes SSNs of 5.8M consumers
item.description: A 2025 data breach at fintech company 700Credit exposes personal information of more than 5.8 million people through compromised third-party integration partner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Using DISM to install Windows updates
item.description: Windows’ built-in Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) command, a.k.a. dism.exe, is something of a Swiss Army knife when it comes to working on Windows OS images.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How this retirement community is using virtual reality to help residents build social connections
item.description: Like many retirement communities, The Terraces serves as a tranquil refuge for a nucleus of older people who no longer can travel to faraway places or engage in bold adventures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company
